Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire process Hiring part 1 Post Job Ad 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets look at Model 1 Activities Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post to Screen Screen to Decide Screen to Interview Decide to Onboard Onboard to Payroll Payroll to Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 1 Make Hiring Decision and Conduct Interviews are both preceded by Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews has no successors It is a dangling activity So you can make a hiring decision before conducting interviews Thats a major logical flaw

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets look at Model 2 Activities Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard in loop Payroll in XOR Close Edges Post to Screen Post to Interview Interview to Decide Decide to loop onboarding loop onboarding to xor payroll xor payroll to Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2 Screen Candidates and Conduct Interviews happen in parallel after Post Job Ad Usually you screen candidates before interviewing them Make Hiring Decision depends on Conduct Interviews but not on Screen Candidates So you can interview and decide without screening

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Screen is a dead end It has no outgoing edges Also the XOR allows skipping Payroll Also the Loop allows Onboard to be repeated

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets re evaluate Model 1 In Model 1 you can go Post to Screen to Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close In this path Interview is never executed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 main path is Post to Interview to Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close This path includes all mandatory steps except Screen AND Payroll can be skipped AND Onboard is in a loop

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Skipping Payroll is a huge anomaly You cannot hire someone and not add them to payroll Therefore Model 1 might be considered more closely aligned because its main successful execution path at least ensures that Payroll is not skipped and Onboard is executed exactly once

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 Anomalies Conduct Interviews is a dead end activity Make Hiring Decision can be executed directly after Screen Candidates bypassing Conduct Interviews entirely

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 Anomalies Screen Candidates is a dead end activity Conduct Interviews can happen independently of Screen Candidates Onboard Employee is wrapped in a Loop operator Add to Payroll is wrapped in an Exclusive Choice with a silent transition This means the process can legally skip adding the new employee to the payroll system entirely before closing the case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is more closely aligned with the normative Hire to Retire process While both models contain structural flaws Model 1 preserves the strict mandatory sequence of the critical administrative downstream activities Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close In Model 1 if a hiring decision is made the employee is guaranteed to be onboarded and added to the payroll before the case is closed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In contrast Model 2 introduces severe control flow anomalies that fundamentally break the business logic of hiring Specifically the XOR operator allows the process to completely skip Add to Payroll which is a mandatory compliance and operational requirement for any new hire Additionally the loop on Onboard Employee suggests redundant onboarding which is nonsensical for a single hiring case
